think:
Okay, let me try to figure out what Sarah should do with her working hours today based on yesterday's performance.

First, I know that Sarah is a Meituan rider who works part-time while studying. Yesterday, she worked from 8 AM to 6 PM. Her rankings for riding distance, money earned, and number of orders were all 15th out of 100 riders. So, she's in the middle but not bad.

I think maybe her work hours are too long because she started at 8 AM and ended at 6 PM. That's a full day without any breaks mentioned. Maybe this is making her tired or less efficient towards the end of the day. If she works such long hours, she might not have enough energy to complete more orders or cover longer distances later on.

Also, if all her metrics are around the middle rank, perhaps she needs to adjust when she starts and stops working. Maybe starting a bit later could help her avoid rush hours or times when there's less demand, allowing her to focus on busier periods instead.

So, maybe cutting down her working hours a bit would make her more productive during peak times. If she works shorter but more efficient shifts, she might actually increase the number of orders and earnings without getting as tired. 

Looking at other riders who performed better than her, they probably have different working hours that allow them to catch more deliveries when demand is high. Sarah could try starting a bit later in the morning and ending earlier in the evening. For example, starting at 10 AM and ending at 4 PM gives her six hours of work without overlapping too much with the peak early morning or late evening rushes.

This way, she can rest more, have better focus during her shifts, and possibly increase her efficiency and earnings. It might also give her time to study or take care of other responsibilities since she's a part-time student.

So, I think adjusting her hours to 10 AM start and 4 PM end could be beneficial for her performance and overall well-being.
